visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.

target.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header.

target.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header before any external connection.

target.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Identify TX, RX, GND, and VCC on DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header; measure idle logic high and run loopback or safe serial capture before target connection.

target.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Map USB-C connector: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-C connector.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C connector before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C connector to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
For CH340C USB-to-Serial bridge IC, measure continuity from USB pins to the USB connector and from UART pins to candidate headers before treating it as a USB serial adapter.
For AP2112 voltage regulator, identify input, output, and ground, then measure no-short, output voltage, load current, and thermal behavior.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path CH340C USB-to-Serial bridge IC to DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.

to.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Continuity-test the visual candidate path CH340C USB-to-Serial bridge IC to USB-C connector; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path AP2112 voltage regulator to DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.

to.label: DTR RXI TXO VCC CTS GND header

visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Continuity-test the visual candidate path AP2112 voltage regulator to USB-C connector; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.

authority_closure.claim_boundary: This closure packet reports what measured evidence unlocks. It does not convert reference pinouts, visual geometry, or model text into physical repair authority.

topology_authority.claim_boundary: Public reference topology can guide tests but does not prove this physical board.

connectors.label: USB-C connector logical pins from schematic

topology_netlist_compilation.claim_boundary: Compiled netlists cover low-voltage measured rails, explicit load models, optional trace/regulator models, and voltage constraints only. Hidden nets, high-speed behavior, batteries, mains, and unmeasured loads remain outside this deterministic DC model.

issues.detail: The compiler emitted rail constraints, but source-current and loaded voltage behavior need measured current or explicit load models.
issues.summary: No numeric load-current model was found.

load_envelope.reason: No source current limit was supplied, so the compiler cannot derive a bounded load envelope without guessing.

topology_authority.claim_boundary: Public reference topology can guide tests but does not prove this physical board.

capture_integrity.claim_boundary: Integrity covers measurement packet auditability only; functional outcome and release manifest are separate gates.

claim_boundary: Session progress means the operator packet is becoming complete. It does not itself authorize power, splice, or repair; authority comes from measured topology closure and downstream outcome/release gates.
